text: {"claims":[{"statement":"Attaining an optimal vitamin D status can be challenging since few foods are natural sources.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Attaining an optimal vitamin D status can be challenging since few foods are natural sources","entailment":"The quoted sentence directly states that achieving optimal vitamin D status is difficult because few foods naturally contain it.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"In all countries above 37 degrees latitude, sun‑induced vitamin D synthesis cannot occur during autumn and winter months.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"in all countries above 37 degrees latitude, sun-induced vitamin D synthesis cannot occur during autumn and winter months","entailment":"The quote explicitly notes the inability of sun‑induced synthesis in those latitudes during those seasons.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Dietary sources of vitamin D, including supplements, are critical for populations with darker skin pigmentation, limited sun exposure, or who cover their skin for religious or cultural reasons.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"dietary sources of vitamin D, including supplements, are critical","entailment":"The sentence identifies dietary vitamin D as essential for the specified groups, supporting the claim.","population":"populations with darker skin pigmentation, limited sun exposure, or who cover their skin for religious or cultural reasons","intervention":"dietary sources of vitamin D, including supplements","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Mean vitamin D intake from food was just 2.7 μg in women and 3.3 μg in men.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"mean vitamin D intake from food was just 2.7 μg in women and 3.3 μg in men","entailment":"The quoted values give the average intake for women and men, directly supporting the statement.","population":"women; men","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"vitamin D intake from food (μg)","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"2.7 μg for women, 3.3 μg for men","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"The current UK recommendation for vitamin D is 10 μg per day.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"current UK recommendation of 10 μg per day","entailment":"The quote states the official UK recommendation, defining the amount.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"10 μg per day","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"The new Irish recommendation for vitamin D is 15 μg per day.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"new Irish recommendation of 15 μg per day","entailment":"The sentence provides the Irish guideline, defining the recommended intake.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"15 μg per day","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"Eggs are one of the few foods naturally high in vitamin D.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"eggs are one of the few foods naturally high in vitamin D","entailment":"The quote directly classifies eggs as a naturally high‑vitamin‑D food.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"In a 12‑week RCT with 51 Australian adults, serum 25(OH)-vitamin D levels significantly reduced only in the group consuming 2 eggs per week.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"after 12-weeks of either 2, 7 or 14 eggs/week during winter months, serum 25(OH)-vitamin D levels had significantly reduced only in the group consuming the lowest number of eggs","entailment":"The quoted result reports a significant reduction in vitamin D levels exclusively for the lowest‑egg group, supporting the claim.","population":"51 Australian adults","intervention":"2 eggs per week (lowest dose)","comparator":"7 or 14 eggs per week","outcome":"serum 25(OH)-vitamin D levels","timeframe":"12 weeks","quantifiers":"2 eggs/week (lowest), 7 or 14 eggs/week (higher)","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Consuming between 7–14 eggs per week may help attenuate the typical seasonal drop in circulating 25(OH)-vitamin D.","claimType":"predictive","quote":"consuming between 7–14 eggs/week may help to attenuate the typical seasonal drop in circulating 25(OH)-vitamin D","entailment":"The sentence suggests a potential benefit of that egg intake range on seasonal vitamin D decline.","population":"","intervention":"7–14 eggs per week","comparator":"","outcome":"seasonal drop in circulating 25(OH)-vitamin D","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"7–14 eggs/week","modality":"conditional"},{"statement":"89 % of women of childbearing age had a red blood cell folate concentration below the threshold indicating increased risk for neural tube defects (748 nmol/L).","claimType":"empirical","quote":"89% of women of childbearing age had a red blood cell folate concentration below the threshold indicating increased risk for NTDs (748 nmol/L)","entailment":"The quoted statistic provides the proportion of women below the risk threshold, directly supporting the claim.","population":"women of childbearing age","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"red blood cell folate concentration below 748 nmol/L","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"89 %","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Intakes of 400 μg of folate are deemed more appropriate for adults aged ≥65 years than the current UK recommendation of 200 μg.","claimType":"value","quote":"intakes of 400 μg were deemed more appropriate for this age group than the current UK recommendation of 200 μg","entailment":"The quote compares the suggested intake with the existing recommendation, indicating a value judgment.","population":"adults aged ≥65 years","intervention":"400 μg folate intake","comparator":"current UK recommendation of 200 μg","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"400 μg vs 200 μg","modality":"normative"},{"statement":"Low folate status in older people is associated with neurological damage and potentially increased risk of dementia.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"increased risk of deficiency in older people, which is associated with neurological damage and potentially increased risk of dementia","entailment":"The sentence links deficiency in older adults to neurological outcomes, supporting the association claim.","population":"older people","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"neurological damage and potential dementia risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"associational"},{"statement":"Health‑conscious egg consumers (consuming >3 eggs/week and low intake of red or processed meat) had significantly higher intakes of folate than those consuming high amounts of red and processed meats and no eggs.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"‘health conscious’ egg consumers (consuming > 3 eggs/week and low intake of red or processed meat) had significantly higher intakes of folate than those consuming high amounts of red and processed meats and no eggs","entailment":"The quoted comparison shows higher folate intake among the specified egg‑eating group versus the high‑meat/no‑egg group.","population":"health‑conscious egg consumers vs high‑meat/no‑egg consumers","intervention":">3 eggs/week with low red/processed meat intake","comparator":"high red/processed meat intake and no eggs","outcome":"folate intake","timeframe":"","quantifiers":">3 eggs/week","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"Female egg consumers had significantly higher intake of folate, protein, and omega‑3 fatty acids.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"female consumers of eggs had significantly higher intake of folate as well as protein and omega-3 fatty acids","entailment":"The quote reports higher nutrient intakes among women who eat eggs, supporting the claim.","population":"female egg consumers","intervention":"egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"intake of folate, protein, omega‑3 fatty acids","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Average European iodine intake in females is estimated to be 127 μg, which is lower than the WHO recommendation of 150 μg/day.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Average European intake of iodine in females is estimated to be 127 μg, which is lower than the World Health Organisation (WHO) recommendation of 150 μg/day","entailment":"The quoted figures provide the estimated intake and the WHO benchmark, showing the shortfall.","population":"European females","intervention":"","comparator":"WHO recommendation of 150 μg/day","outcome":"iodine intake (μg)","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"127 μg vs 150 μg","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"Iodine is an essential nutrient for normal foetal brain development and a low status may impact fertility and the risk of preeclampsia.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"Iodine is an essential nutrient for normal foetal brain development and a low status may impact fertility and the risk of preeclampsia","entailment":"The sentence defines iodine’s role and potential consequences of deficiency.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"foetal brain development, fertility, preeclampsia risk","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"Long‑term iodine intake from food sources may be more important for optimal behavioural outcomes in infants than iodine supplementation during pregnancy.","claimType":"predictive","quote":"long-term iodine intake from food sources may be more important for optimal behavioural outcomes in infants than iodine supplementation during pregnancy","entailment":"The quote predicts a greater benefit of dietary iodine over supplementation for infant outcomes.","population":"infants","intervention":"long‑term iodine intake from food sources","comparator":"iodine supplementation during pregnancy","outcome":"optimal behavioural outcomes","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"predictive"},{"statement":"Eggs, being high in iodine, could provide a useful addition to the diet both prior to and during pregnancy to aid sufficient iodine intake.","claimType":"predictive","quote":"As eggs are high in iodine, they could provide a useful addition to the diet both prior to and during pregnancy to aid sufficient iodine intake","entailment":"The sentence suggests that egg consumption may help meet iodine needs around pregnancy.","population":"people prior to and during pregnancy","intervention":"egg consumption","comparator":"","outcome":"iodine intake","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"predictive"},{"statement":"Eggs are considered a source of high‑quality protein because they contain all nine essential amino acids.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"Eggs are considered a source of high-quality protein since they contribute all nine essential amino acids","entailment":"The quote defines why eggs qualify as high‑quality protein.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"Objective measures of protein quality and digestibility give a high value for eggs (97%).","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Objective measures of protein quality and digestibility give a high value for eggs (97%)","entailment":"The quoted measurement assigns a high protein quality score to eggs.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"protein quality value (97%)","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"97%","modality":"descriptive"},{"statement":"High‑quality protein consumed frequently across the day supports myoprotection in combination with appropriate exercise, which helps prevent loss of skeletal muscle mass and function in older adults.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"High-quality protein consumed frequently across the day supports myoprotection in combination with appropriate exercise, which helps to prevent loss of skeletal muscle mass and function in older adults","entailment":"The sentence links frequent high‑quality protein intake plus exercise to protection against muscle loss in older adults.","population":"older adults","intervention":"high‑quality protein consumed frequently across the day plus appropriate exercise","comparator":"","outcome":"prevention of loss of skeletal muscle mass and function","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"","modality":"causal"},{"statement":"The current protein recommendation for adults, as proposed by EFSA, is 0.83 g/kg body weight daily.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"current protein recommendation for adults, as proposed by EFSA, is 0.83 g/kg body weight daily","entailment":"The quote states the EFSA guideline, defining the recommended amount.","population":"adults","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"protein recommendation (g/kg body weight)","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"0.83 g/kg body weight daily","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"A recent paper recommends 1.2 g/kg body weight daily, equating to 84 g for a 70 kg person.","claimType":"definitional","quote":"recommendation made in the aforementioned paper is for 1.2 g/kg body weight daily, equating to 84 g for a 70 kg person","entailment":"The sentence provides the alternative protein recommendation and its conversion for a 70 kg individual.","population":"","intervention":"","comparator":"","outcome":"protein recommendation (g/kg body weight)","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"1.2 g/kg body weight daily; 84 g for 70 kg","modality":"definitional"},{"statement":"Two medium eggs provide 12.8 g of high‑quality protein, contributing significantly to protein recommendations.","claimType":"empirical","quote":"Two medium eggs provide 12.8 g of high-quality protein which would make an important contribution to protein recommendations","entailment":"The quoted amount quantifies protein from two eggs and notes its relevance to dietary guidelines.","population":"","intervention":"two medium eggs","comparator":"","outcome":"high‑quality protein (g)","timeframe":"","quantifiers":"12.8 g","modality":"descriptive"}]}
